articles.title: .@PatrickRuffini finds the 5.3pp shift in Georgia towards the Democratic presidential candidate from 2016 to 2020 was driven by vote switching from third party or Trump to Biden by 3.1pp, and demographic change worth 2.2pp.
articles.author: Patrick Ruffini

articles.title: Since 2017, commercial real estate insurance costs have grown at 7.6% per year. Developers report that new projects are sometimes seeing no insurance bids.
articles.author: Konrad Putzier

articles.title: Looking at postwar data, researchers at @sffed find that there was a regime shift around 1999 towards well-anchored inflation expectations that were not sensitive to incoming CPI data.
articles.author: Kevin Lansing and Federico Nucera
articles.pub: Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco

articles.title: A Bloomberg analysis found that, for 88 S&P 100 companies, 94% of job growth in 2021 went to non-white workers.
articles.author: Jeff Green, David Ingold, Raeedah Wahid, Cedric Sam and Daniela Sirtori-Cortina

articles.title: US equities account for nearly 70% of the MSCI World index, and the 10 largest US equities are larger than the combined market capitalization of Japan, the UK, France, Canada, and Germany. European-based firms are looking at listing on US exchanges.
articles.author: Philip Augar

articles.title: According to @lee_ohanian @AEIecon, labor market conflict explains half of the decline in the Rust Belt's share of total manufacturing employment between 1950 and 2000.
articles.author: Simeon Alder, David Lagakos, and Lee Ohanian

articles.title: Americans and Britons have shifted from believing “wealth can grow so there’s enough for everyone” to “people can only get rich at the expense of others.” Democrats who voted for Trump in 2016 scored very high on zero-sum beliefs. @jburnmurdoch
articles.author: John Burn-Murdoch

articles.title: A @federalreserve analysis from last year finds the manufacturing wage premium has disappeared for most manufacturing jobs as of April 2018. The decline in unionization is responsible for "more than 70% of the drop in the manufacturing wage premium."
articles.author: Kimberly Bayard, Tomaz Cajner, Vivi Gregorich, and Maria Tito

articles.title: Zero-sum thinking in terms of political and policy views is strongly associated with lower levels of intergenerational upward mobility. @S_Stantcheva @DrNathanNunn
articles.author: Sahil Chinoy, Nathan Nunn, Sandra Sequeira and Stefanie Stantcheva

articles.title: 32mm foreign-born workers made up 18% of the American labor force last year, the highest level since the series was initially published in 1996.
articles.author: Amara Omeokwe and Michelle Hackman

articles.title: Stabilizing US federal debt at 100% of GDP will require increased tax revenue and non-interest spending cuts of 5% of GDP going forward. @Brian_Riedl suggests that increasing tax rates on the rich could yield at most 2% of GDP and likely less.
articles.author: Brian Riedl

articles.title: .@FedGuy12 writes that Treasury liquidity is low as dealer balance sheets have not scaled up with Treasury issuance. The average daily volume of Treasuries has increased very slowly over the past decade despite a flood of issuance.
articles.author: Joseph Wang

articles.title: Recent studies in the US, Sweden, and Finland demonstrate that new unsubsidized housing construction enhances overall affordability by freeing up housing for people with lower incomes. @jburnmurdoch
articles.author: John Burn-Murdoch

articles.title: Median family incomes have declined every year since 2019; however, @jmhorp notes that median black household income is now the highest it's ever been.
articles.author: Jeremy Horpedahl

articles.title: US median household income fell in real terms by 2.1% in 2022 for the third straight year, as inflation outpaced wage gains.  Since its pre-pandemic peak, real household income has fallen by almost 5%.
articles.author: Gwynn Guilford and Paul Overberg

articles.title: .@kearney_melissa argues that the rise of single-parent households is leading to lower social mobility for children raised in those households relative to two-parent households.
articles.author: David Price and Melissa Kearney

articles.title: New @federalreserve research demonstrates elevated corporate profit margins during the pandemic period were a function of government spending and lower interest expenses.
articles.author: Berardino Palazzo

articles.title: Western countries are facing tradeoffs to finance increased defense spending as the post-Cold War "peace dividend" fades in the face of Chinese and Russian revanchism, with 20 of 31 NATO members still below the 2% of GDP target.
articles.author: John Paul Rathbone

articles.title: Hendrik Bessembinder finds that the best-performing 1,526 global firms (2.4% of total) accounted for all of the $75.7T in net global stock market wealth creation between 1990 and 2020.
articles.author: Hendrik Bessembinder, Te-Feng Chen, Goeun Choi and K.C. John Wei

articles.title: Seven stocks made up 70% of S&P 500 value creation in 2023, but Jesper Rangvid argues that this isn’t without historical precedent.  In the Dot.com period, Cisco, Dell, Intel, Lucent, and Microsoft had an even more extreme run-up.
articles.author: Jesper Rangvid

articles.title: .@Nate_Cohn argues that current polling suggests a significant decline in black turnout in 2024 relative to 2020.
articles.author: Nate Cohn

articles.title: Racial polarization in voting has been declining since 2012 in the US.  @Nate_Cohn finds Biden underperforming relative to 2020 in current polling with non-white voters; 5% of 2020 non-white Biden voters now say they support Trump.
articles.author: Nate Cohn

articles.title: The average hourly wage for auto workers has dropped 30% since 2003 and has converged with non-auto production workers.  The UAW is attempting to arrest the trend, but the Big Three's profits would be erased if they meet the UAW’s demands. @foxjust
articles.author: Justin Fox

articles.title: Researchers at @sffed find that an unexpected 1% rise in short-term interest rates reduces real GDP by 5% after 12 years, but no evidence that loose monetary policy increases potential output. @sanjayrajsingh
articles.author: Òscar Jordà, Sanjay Singh and Alan Taylor
articles.pub: Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco

articles.title: The growth in Medicare spending per beneficiary leveled off nearly a decade ago. If spending had grown at the prior rate, spending since 2011 would have been at least $3.9 trillion higher.
articles.author: Margot Sanger-Katz, Alicia Parlapiano and Josh Katz

articles.title: The typical Asian-American household in the US earns just over $100,000, 3x a typical Japanese family's earnings.
articles.author: Megumi Fujikawa

articles.title: Capital inflows into North American markets have contributed to a 3.9x Price/Book value relative to market averages of 1.9x in Europe and 1.4x in Japan according to @verdadcap_quant.  He believes this offers opportunities in both Europe and Japan.
articles.author: Brian Chingono

articles.title: According to a @BudgetHawks analysis the US budget deficit will double in 2023 despite economic growth.  @jasonfurman notes such deficits typically are associated with a "major crisis" like World War II or the 2008 financial meltdown.
articles.author: Jeff Stein

articles.title: Since 2018, auto manufacturers have announced at least $110 billion of EV-related domestic investment, about half in Southern states, driven by lower labor and energy costs. Unionizing southern auto workers is a priority for the UAW.
articles.author: Nora Eckert

articles.title: Alan Auerbach @Kotlikoff argue that the global savings glut was a "myth."  The market return on capital, which would show a decline if there were a capital glut, increased in the 2000s and 2010s.
articles.author: Alan Auerbach and Larry Kotlikoff

articles.title: Ideologically driven donors of $200 or less are driving political polarization in the US, as small donors hold far more extreme views than those of the mean voter. @Edsall
articles.author: Thomas Edsall

articles.title: The labor force participation rate for prime-age women (ages 25-54) is at an all-time high 77.8%, driven by mothers whose youngest child is under 5 years old. @laurenlbauer
articles.author: Lauren Bauer and Sarah Yu Wang

articles.title: The German economic model is under increasing stress due to higher energy costs, China's import substitution strategy, and competition from American and Chinese electric car makers.
articles.author: Bojan Pancevski, Paul Hannon and William Boston

articles.title: .@GoldmanSachs finds that 20-25% of US workers are working from home at least part of the week, well above the pre-pandemic average of 2.6%. This exerts upward pressure on office vacancy rates, partially offset by a decline in new office construction.
articles.author: Jan Hatzius and Others

articles.title: Historically monetary tightening has had an impact on risk capital: 100bps of tightening is associated with a 1-3pp decline in R&D spending and a 25% decline in VC investment over the following 1-3 year period.
articles.author: Yueran Ma and Kaspar Zimmermann
articles.pub: Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City

articles.title: The decline in the college wage premium has been driven by faster wage growth for high school workers rather than slower wage growth for college graduates. @sffed
articles.author: Leila Bengali, Marcus Sander, Robert G. Valletta and Cindy Zhao
articles.pub: Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco

articles.title: Since 2007, the ratio of Treasuries outstanding to primary dealer assets has increased by a factor of four.  @DuffieDarrell argues that this will drive increasing illiquidity in the Treasury market.
articles.author: Darrell Duffie
articles.pub: Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City

articles.title: .@fuxianyi expects fewer than eight million newborns in China this year, based on indicators including marriage and newborn-medical-checkup data, less than half the number in 2016 when China scrapped its one-child policy.

articles.title: .@charlesmurray argues that urban disorder has increased since 2013, when cities abandoned broken-windows policing.

articles.title: .@JohnHCochrane takes a victory lap, arguing that the fiscal theory of the price level is the most descriptive theory of inflation.

articles.title: The 13M Americans working in the manufacturing sector in January surpassed the peak set during the previous business cycle in February of 2020, a first since the 1970s. This growth has been accompanied by a decline in real output per hour worked. @foxjust

articles.title: Elite students overperform on admission tests due to selection for "good luck." Harvard undergrads have a mean IQ of 130, adjusted for test obsolescence & sample bias, vs 145 on the SAT.
articles.author: Pumpkin Person

articles.title: Warnock outspent Walker nearly 3 to 1, with $126.2m in expenditures compared to Walker's $48.5m. Warnock also knocked on 4mm doors, outpacing Walker's 400,000.
articles.author: Victoria Marshall

articles.title: Evidence from Norway shows internet expansion reduced friction associated with finding a job and reduced the steady state unemployment rate by 14%. @AndreasKostol @nberpubs
articles.author: Manudeep Bhuller, Domenico Ferraro, et al

articles.title: Michael Spence and Belinda Azenui note that labor productivity between the tradable and non-tradable sectors started to diverge in 1998, and by 2021 per-employee value-added in tradable sectors was nearly double the non-tradable sectors. @ProSyn
articles.author: Michael Spence and Belinda Azenui

articles.title: .@generaltheorist notes that during the pandemic for every additional $1T in currency and deposits, American households saw $6T in non-monetary wealth driven by equities and real estate.
articles.author: Chris Marsh

articles.title: .@TheEconomist reports that between mid-2020-2022 America's lower earners saw cumulative inflation of 13.5% whereas middle earners saw 15.5%.  The difference was largely driven by car prices and fuel expenditures. @XJaravel
articles.author: Economist Staff

articles.title: Evidence from Sweden finds a strong relationship between cognitive ability and earnings up to annual earnings of ~$64k. There are no significant differences among the top three income percentiles.  @ECSR_Soc
articles.author: Marc Keuschnigg, Arnout van de Rijt and Thijs Bol

articles.title: Matt Rognlie @ludwigstraub @a_auclert argue that it will take up to five years for excess savings to work though the systems as individuals with higher marginal propensity to consume generate income for individuals with higher savings rates.
articles.author: Adrien Auclert, Matthew Rognlie and Ludwig Straub

articles.title: .@mattsclancy highlights evidence that major productivity gains in research result from allowing scientists & researchers to cluster with other highly skilled inventors. Allowing more high-skilled immigration could boost per capita GDP growth rates by 9%.
articles.author: Matt Clancy

articles.title: Public firms’ returns on the book value of assets are down ~ 50% from 1980 and private firms' returns have doubled. @EconTraina @ASollaci @CarterDavisFin
articles.author: Carter Davis, Alexandre Sollaci and James Traina

articles.title: The @economist notes that “almost all” recent AI breakthroughs have come from large firms, in large part due to their access to the computing power required to develop state-of-the-art AI models.
articles.author: Economist Staff

articles.title: The aging US population has resulted in a shortfall of 1.9m workers since 2019, with a decline in LFP among older Americans accounting for an additional 500,000 workers lost.
articles.author: Elliott Morris and Simon Rabinovitch

articles.title: .@EthanYWu offers support to the Charles Goodhart and Manoj Pradhan view that population aging is inflationary as retirees create more demand than supply.
articles.author: Ethan Wu

articles.title: .@Jasonfurman notes GDP is "basically at CBO's pre-pandemic forecast." Consumer spending, which has enjoyed "huge fiscal support in 2020 and 2021,” is running well ahead of the CBO's pre-pandemic forecast, while residential investment declined by 26.7%/yr.
articles.author: Jason Furman

articles.title: According to @GoldmanSachs, foreign-born labor force is rebounding from 2.8mm below trend in September 2020 to 1.2mm below trend in March 2022.
articles.author: Tim Krupa

articles.title: .@Edsall argues that a “white rural red wave” is the driving force of the Republican party today and cites shifts in Wisconsin suburban and rural voting patterns between 2016 and 2022.
articles.author: Thomas Edsall

articles.title: According to a new @USCBO forecast, US population growth after 2033 will be driven by immigration, which will account for all American population growth in 2042. @Bloomberg
articles.author: Alexandre Tanzi

articles.title: The aging of the American population has left the US short 1.9M workers relative to 2019.  A decline in labor force participation of older workers is responsible for a drop of another 500,000 workers. @gelliottmorris @S_Rabinovitch
articles.author: G. Elliott Morris and Simon Rabinovitch

articles.title: A @nberpubs analysis finds workers who are working from home in the US save an average of 55 minutes with 39% of those time savings being devoted to a primary or secondary jobs and 33% for leisure activities.  @I_Am_NickBloom
articles.author: Cevat Giray Aksoy, Jose Maria Barrero, Nicholas Bloom, et al

articles.title: Michael Cembalest @jpmorgan notes that it has been difficult for legislators to increase tax receipts beyond 19% of GDP, and yet entitlement spending plus interest is likely to exceed that level by 2032.
articles.author: Michael Cembalest

articles.title: .@ojblanchard1 argues that the secular stagnation environment – where real interest rates were low, often negative, and much lower than the economy’s rate of growth – will soon reassert itself and will then be the "prevalent regime for some time to come."
articles.author: Olivier Blanchard

articles.title: .@WSJ reports that year-over-year wages for the bottom 10% of earners increased 9.8% relative to 7.4% for the median for all workers.  Workers in the top 10% of earnings saw a 5.7% gain.
articles.author: Gabriel Rubin

articles.title: Tim Krupa from @GoldmanSachs reports that the foreign-born labor force is rebounding from 2.8M below trend in September 2020 to 1.2M below trend in March 2022.
articles.author: Tim Krupa

articles.title: Last year @Brian_Riedl compared Trump, Obama, and George W. Bush’s fiscal records and found that Trump’s policies in one term cost $7.8T over a decade, relative to $5T for Obama and $6.9T for Bush.
articles.author: Brian Riedl

articles.title: .@Brian_Riedl offers practical suggestions on how Republicans could permanently stabilize the American debt at 95% of GDP, a goal that would start with running deficits at 3% of GDP relative to the current baseline of 6% over the next decade.
articles.author: Brian Riedl

articles.title: Charles Blahous @mercatus notes that America's long-term Federal fiscal imbalance was largely set between 1965-72. The cost of programs enacted during that period is growing faster than economic output and neither party has made an effort to address this.
articles.author: Charles Blahous

articles.title: .@JBSay notes there have been at least 400,000 unexpected deaths among the US working-age population since 2020. Netting out Covid deaths and unnatural deaths (homicide, suicide, overdose, etc.) he finds a spike starting in 2021.
articles.author: Bret Swanson

articles.title: @BretSwanson notes at least 400,000 unexpected deaths among working-age US population since pandemic start, excluding Covid-19 & unnatural causes. Mortality rates for young & middle-aged workers surged, contributing to 3.5m labor force gap.
articles.author: Bret Swanson

articles.title: Raj Chetty and his @OppInsights team find that America's workforce earning <$29k/year is down about 20% relative to its January 2020 level. Adjusting for workers now earning over $29k, the low-wage workforce was 13.5% under its pre-pandemic level. @BSteverman
articles.author: Ben Steverman

articles.title: Americans’ workforce earning <$29,000 a year is down 20% relative to January 2020 level; adjusted for workers earning more than $29K, workforce is down 13.5%.
articles.author: Ben Steverman

articles.title: According to a new analysis from the @stlouisfed, job switching is associated with lower earnings growth for lower-earning prime-age male workers but higher earnings growth for their higher-income peers.
articles.author: Serdar Ozkan and Cassandra Marks
articles.pub: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis

articles.title: The uptick in so-called "deaths of despair" was preceded by a decline in religious participation. @econburner @D_Hungerman @TamarOostrom offer evidence that the repeal of blue laws is associated with the rise of such deaths.
articles.author: Tyler Giles, Daniel Hungerman, and Tamar Oostrom

articles.title: Value-added/full-time employee in the US construction sector was ~40% lower in 2020 than in 1970; had construction productivity grown at 1% a year, aggregate US labor productivity would have been 10% higher.  @Austan_Goolsbee @ChadSyverson @nberpubs
articles.author: Austan Goolsbee and Chad Syverson

articles.title: New @nberpubs notes that the demand for safe assets from 1990-2020 grew even faster than the supply of safe assets, as rapidly growing emerging economies increasing desired to hold safe assets as FX reserves.
articles.author: Enrique Mendoza and Vincenzo Quadrini

articles.title: .@joshrauh of the Stanford Graduate School of Business presents evidence that migration from California is being driven by high taxes rates, suggesting California has limited ability to raise taxes without losing revenue.
articles.author: Joshua Rauh

articles.title: Firms are using technology to shift work to remote employees and third-party subcontractors. Outsourcing intensity has doubled from 11% in 2005 to 22% in 2021, which may compress white-collar wages going forward. @TheEconomist
articles.author: Economist Staff

articles.title: .@ATabarrok uses semiconductor manufacturing to argue that properly trained talent is the limiting constraint to growth at the technological frontier.  He notes that only 164,000 potential American workers have the IQ needed to manage the process.
articles.author: Alex Tabarrok

articles.title: Research papers & patents have become less disruptive over time. Btw 1945 & 2010, mean disruptiveness of papers declined 91.9%-100%, patents 78.7%-91.5%.
articles.author: Michael Park, Erin Leahey and Russell Funk

articles.title: New @nberpubs research finds that Americans are working 3% fewer hours annually in the aftermath of the pandemic.  This reduction in hours worked means labor markets are even tighter than LFP would imply.
articles.author: Dain Lee, Jinhyeok Park and Yongseok Shin

articles.title: A new @nature study documents a decline in the average "disruptiveness" of science and technology across both papers and patents, but notes that the absolute number of highly disruptive works remains stable. @michae1park
articles.author: Michael Park, Erin Leahey and Russell Funk

articles.title: Phil Gramm and John Early report on @MichaelRStrain analysis that found robust absolute mobility in America. Only 28% of children raised in the bottom quintile had adult incomes that were in the same quintile and 26% reached the top quintile. @AEIecon
articles.author: Phil Gramm and John Early

articles.title: .@mfariacastro at @stlouisfed estimates that the decline in asset values in 2022 drove 170,000 workers aged 51-65 back into the labor force. This represents 16% of the increase in LFP from Jan through Oct 2022.
articles.author: Miguel Faria e Castro and Sam Jordan-Wood
articles.pub: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis

articles.title: @GwynnGuilford 40% of recently laid-off tech workers accepted a new position within 30 days, according to a December 2022 survey, highlighting the resilience of tech employment despite economic challenges.
articles.author: Gwynn Guilford

articles.title: Capital expenditures net of depreciation for the Russell 3000 peaked at 6.9% of sales in 1988, but has since declined to 1.8% on average in the past decade, highlighting a shift in capital allocation strategies.
articles.author: Michael Mauboussin and Dan Callahan

articles.title: According to recent surveys, recently laid-off tech workers are quickly finding new employment with 40% accepting a new position within 30 days. @WSJ
articles.author: Sarah Chaney Cambon and Gwynn Guilford

articles.title: J.P. Morgan estimates consumers still have $900B of excess savings, down from a peak of $2.1B in August 2021.
articles.author: JP Morgan Staff

articles.title: Independent analyst @JosephPolitano notes that the primary driver of capacity under-utilization for American manufacturing firms has shifted  from insufficient orders to insufficient supply of labor and materials.
articles.author: Joseph Politano

articles.title: .@FiveThirtyEight analyst @geoffreyvs finds that Fetterman’s margin of victory was provided by non-college white voters: Fetterman polled 7pp better than Biden in counties dominated by non-college whites.
articles.author: Geoffrey Skelley

articles.title: According to data from Insider Intelligence, Google and Facebook's share of digital advertising was 48.4% in 2022, and is expected to decline to 44.9% in 2023, as Amazon, TikTok, and digital streamers gain share. @WSJ
articles.author: Patience Haggin

articles.title: .@JohnHCochrane reviews Phil Gramm, Robert Ekelund and John Early book and highlights their big finding, "The effective marginal tax rate in the lowest three quintiles is effectively 100%. Earn a dollar and lose a dollar of benefits. Why work?"
articles.author: John Cochrane

articles.title: During Biden's first two years in office, he has approved at least $4.8T of new borrowing according to @BudgetHawks.
articles.author: CRFB Staff
articles.pub: Center For A Responsible Federal Budget

articles.title: Bank of America notes evidence of reshoring to Mexico at the expense of China with imports of Mexican low-tech goods such as plastics and textiles up ~60% relative to pre-pandemic levels.
articles.author: Stephen Wilmot

articles.title: The American college workforce is 5% larger than in Feb 2022, whereas the high school workforce is 4% smaller. This has likely contributed to the decrease in earnings inequality in the post-pandemic period. @Greg_Ip
articles.author: Greg Ip

articles.title: .@davidautor shows high school workers' wage growth overtaking college wage growth in the aftermath of the pandemic and argues that higher wages better reflect rising productivity as companies compete more intensively for workers.
articles.author: David Autor

articles.title: Americans are drinking at elevated levels in the aftermath of the pandemic with inflation-adjusted spending up 15% in 2022 vs. just before the pandemic. @foxjust
articles.author: Justin Fox

articles.title: cording to @jmhorp Millennials are keeping pace with Baby Boomers and Gen X in terms of generational wealth per capita.
articles.author: Jeremy Horpedahl

articles.title: An analysis by @jburnmurdoch suggests millennials in the US and UK are breaking a historical pattern of becoming more conservative in their voting behavior as they age.
articles.author: John Burn-Murdoch

articles.title: First generation immigrants make up 16% of American inventors, but are responsible for 23% of total innovation output. Including the spillover effects on native born collaborators, they drive 36% of aggregate innovation. @bibipousada @shaibrn @rebeccardiamond
articles.author: Shai Bernstein, Rebecca Diamond, et al

articles.title: First generation immigrants make up 16% of American inventors, but are responsible for 23% of total innovation output. Including the spillover effects on native born collaborators, they drive 36% of aggregate innovation. @bibipousada @shaibrn @rebeccardiamond
articles.author: Joey Politano

articles.title: .@mjmauboussin shows capital expenditures net of depreciation as a percentage of sales peaked in 1988 at 6.9%. Over the past decade it has averaged 1.8% and fell to 0.5% in 2020 during the pandemic.
articles.author: Michael Mauboussin and Dan Callahan

articles.title: Citing CBO data, @MichaelRStrain finds that market income for the median household grew by 26% from 1990 through 2019 after adjusting for inflation. Including transfer payments and federal taxes, real median household income grew 55%. @AEIecon
articles.author: Michael Strain

articles.title: BlackRock analysis argues declining LFP means economic activity ‘will need to run at a lower level to avoid persistent wage and price inflation.’
articles.author: Ethan Wu

articles.title: According to research published by @NBER, higher productivity growth in 1989-2017 “is more than sufficient to explain the greater wage growth that more educated workers enjoyed as compared with less educated workers.”
articles.author: Edward Lazear, Kathryn Shaw, Grant Hayes and James Jedras

articles.title: The retired share of the US population was 1.5pp above pre-pandemic trend in Oct 2022, with 3.5mm retirees accounting for nearly all of the shortfall in LFP rates.
articles.author: Joshua Montes, Christopher Smith, and Juliana Dajon

articles.title: .@johnauthers reports that we may have seen a trough in global liquidity, according to data from Crossborder Capital.
articles.author: John Authers

articles.title: .@swinshi notes that @JacobBastian25’s latest research underestimates the disemployment effects of an enhanced Child Tax Credit. Scott view of the disemployment effects support Bruce Myers and @kefincorinth CTC estimates @UChicago. @aei
articles.author: Scott Winship

articles.title: In October 2022, the retired share of the American population was 1½ percentage points above its pre-pandemic trend. These 3.5 million workers account for essentially all of the shortfall in labor force participation rates. @federalreserve
articles.author: Joshua Montes, Christopher Smith and Juliana Dajon

articles.title: Chinese R&D and equipment upgrade subsidies fail to generate a positive impact on firm-level total factor productivity. @nber
articles.author: Lee Branstetter, Guangwei Li and Mengjia Ren

articles.title: .@Noahpinion reviews the shift of American innovation from corporate labs to university research and startups but notes that Google’s AI divisions have been an important driver of research in the frontier of machine learning innovation.
articles.author: Noah Smith

articles.title: By 2075, India and China are projected to surpass the US in GDP size, yet the US will maintain a significant lead in real per capita GDP, increasing from $69k in 2021 to $132k.
articles.author: Kevin Daly and Tadas Gedminas

articles.title: Only 20% of US workers have seen real hourly earnings growth over the past year, as inflation outpaces wage increases in most sectors, complicating the Fed's task to curb inflation without stifling growth.
articles.author: Karen Dynan

articles.title: .@EthanYWu at @ft shares Blackrock analysis forecasting declining American labor force participation rates driven by aging. This implies that economic activity “will need to run at a lower level to avoid persistent wage and price inflation,"
articles.author: Ethan Wu

articles.title: China's aging population and declining youth share hinder similar political shifts, limiting the potential for large-scale protests necessary for change, suggesting China may struggle to escape the middle-income trap.
articles.author: Yi Fuxian

articles.title: Change in their relative earnings accounted for 44% of the growth in labor force exits among non-college men between 1980-2019, suggesting a decline in social status is a likely factor driving the decline in prime-age labor force participation. @BostonFed
articles.author: Pinghui Wu

articles.title: In a survey of 1,200 millennials aged 26-41, one in four are currently living with their parent, and half of these moved in with family within the past year. @bloomberg
articles.author: Alexandre Tanzi

articles.title: Over the past 12 months, only 20% of American workers have experienced real hourly earnings growth. Outside the leisure and hospitality, information and transportation, and warehousing sectors, wage gains fell short of 5.9% PCE price index inflation. @KarenDynan
articles.author: Karen Dynan and Wilson Powell

articles.title: .@GoldmanSachs forecasts that by 2075 both India and China will have slightly larger GDP than the US, but US real per capita GDP will rise from $69k in 2021 to $132k, vs. 2075 forecasts of $55k for China and $31k for India.
articles.author: Kevin Daly and Tadas Gedminas

articles.title: .@fuxianyi at @ProSyn argues that political transformations tend to occur during a “youth boom” when the share of the population aged 15-29 exceeds 28%. The proportion of youth aged 15-29 in China stood at just 17% last year, and the median age was 42.
articles.author: Yi Fuxian

articles.title: .@AEI’s @swinshi revisits Raj Chetty’s data and argues that contrary to Chetty’s findings, slower economic growth has been a much more important driver of the reduction of absolute mobility than the rise in inequality.
articles.author: Scott Winship

articles.title: 2021 saw a record 48,953 gun deaths or 15 fatalities per 100,000 people. Black men aged 20-24 suffered 142 firearm homicides per 100,000 people in 2021, a 74% increase since 2014. @wsj
articles.author: Dominique Mosbergen

articles.title: .@PaulKrugman suggests that wage growth for lower-income families has more than offset inflation, even accounting for their higher food and energy consumption.
articles.author: Paul Krugman

articles.title: There has been a sharp uptick in the number of men working as stay-at-home parents. Almost 5% of stay-at-home parents are men, compared to about 1% in the mid-1990s.  @bloomberg @jordan_yadoo
articles.author: Jordan Yadoo

articles.title: China’s trade surplus has continued to grow since the pandemic, with manufacturing exports ~ 14% of GDP. The trade surplus is poised to grow, as China's bill for imported commodities falls. @Brad_Setser

articles.title: Young adults in both the UK and US are spending more time alone and increasingly reporting feeling “lonely.” @ft
articles.author: Federica Cocco

articles.title: Research by @RolandFryer suggests skill disparities rather than racial bias drive wage differences, with AFQT score accounting for 71% of black-white age gap among young men.

articles.title: The MSCI India Index has outperformed the MSCI China Index through April 2022, according to @Wellington_Mgmt research, as noted by @tylercowen
articles.author: Niraj Bhagwat and Philip Brooks

articles.title: A 2019 RAND study found 60% of American men in the 26-35 cohort who failed to graduate from high school had been arrested by age 26, and their arrest rate has been going up over time, reports @adam_tooze

articles.author: Ruy Teixeira

articles.title: Btw Q1 2020 and Q2 2022, US housing wealth surged by nearly $9tn, driven by price gains rather than an increase in housing stock. A 15-20% price correction could reduce real personal consumption expenditures by 0.5-0.7pp.

articles.title: @WilliamBarr @WSJ Btw 1991-2013 total prison population in the US doubled from 800,000 to 1.6mm; during that period, violent crime was cut in half.

articles.title: Education spending effectiveness confirmed: 92% of multi-state studies (12/13) show significant positive outcome correlation. Meta-analysis validates causal link btw funding and student achievement.

articles.title: The productivity boost during the pandemic might be a mirage, driven by cyclical effects rather than sustained innovation.
articles.author: John Fernald

articles.author: OECD Staff

articles.title: According to @AmyGlasmeier @MIT, a household of one adult and two children in NYC requires an hourly wage of $49.18 to meet basic living expenses.

articles.title: The inspection paradox distorts sample data by overrepresenting larger groups, leading to biased averages: e.g., average class sizes appear 90 vs. an actual 35, and prison sentences average 13 years vs. an actual 3.6 years, skewing perceptions.

articles.title: The US allocates 2.8% of GDP to R&D, yet evidence suggests this is insufficient, impacting living standards, health, and national competitiveness.

articles.title: No “Great Demographic Reversal”: population aging will likely continue to push down r through 2100.
articles.author: Adrien Auclert

articles.title: The latest IPCC report indicates a revised climate sensitivity range of 2.5 to 4 degrees Celsius, higher than the late 1800s, reflecting a moderated top-end estimate compared to the 2013 range of 1.5 to 4.5 degrees. @WSJ.

articles.title: Expanded UI had a modest job search disincentive, $600 reduced employment by <.8%, $300 reduced employment by <.5%.
articles.author: Fiona Greig

articles.title: Large manufacturing plant openings boost local productivity, with employment & wages increasing by 3.5% & 3.7% in the winner county. Knowledge spillovers drive gains, particularly in industries with mutual R&D flows & patent citations. @nberpub

articles.title: U.S. energy intensity has dropped by half since 1983, reaching a low of 5.05 thousand Btu per chained 2012 dollar in 2020, down 4% from 2019. Energy intensity varies significantly by state, with LA, WY, WV exhibiting higher energy intensity.

articles.title: Carbon-based fuels provided 81% of American total energy demand in 2020, and 71% of electrical demand.

articles.title: Chinese solar panel production creates twice as much carbon dioxide as panels manufactured in Europe.
articles.author: Matthew Dalton

articles.title: 33 of 37 OECD countries require photo IDs to vote, contrasting with U.S. debates on voter ID laws. Europe’s strict ID policies aim to prevent fraud and boost turnout.

articles.title: By 2100, the average person worldwide is projected to be 436% better off compared to 450% without climate change, indicating a modest reduction in prosperity growth.
articles.author: Bjorn Lomborg

articles.title: @BenjaminZycher Reducing US fossil fuel production benefits OPEC+, offsetting US cuts with increased production. This shift transfers wealth to foreign producers, highlighting the unintended consequences of US policy decisions.
articles.author: Benjamin Zycher

articles.title: National real house prices rose 80% from 1997 to 2006, lost 2/3 of their gain by 2012, then rebounded, illustrating a boom-bust-rebound cycle.

articles.title: Markups and intangible capital explain somewhere btw 1/3 to 2/3 of the measured productivity slowdown during the 2000s.

articles.title: Low-skilled job wage growth outpaced overall pay in June, reflecting a shift in labor market dynamics. @JordynHolman reports, citing Kroger's average hourly rate expected to reach $21 by year-end.

articles.title: The Shleifer model suggests financial crises are more predictable than traditionally believed, particularly when examining periods of rapid credit & asset price growth. The probability of a financial crisis within one year of entering the Red Zone is over 13%, compared to a 4% baseline.
articles.author: Greg Obenshain

articles.title: Revamp of climate change models as current models are projecting rates that are implausibly fast. The IPCC plans to use actual warming data to constrain CMIP projections, potentially reducing uncertainty by 50% and lowering extreme projections.

articles.title: Research by @ErikHurst highlights task-based discrimination, where Black workers are underrepresented in high-paying Abstract tasks, widening the racial wage gap since 1980.

articles.title: @JordanWeissmann Masters degrees have become a lucrative venture for universities, with students accumulating unsustainable debt. Stronger regulation is needed, including a cap on borrowing & loan forgiveness, to prevent excessive student debt.
articles.author: Jordan Weissmann

articles.title: Germany's job market has seen a significant shift since 1995, with nearly all new jobs being part-time, highlighting a structural issue in its labor market. @M_C_Klein suggests addressing this issue could provide Germany with additional fiscal space under the Schuldenbremse.

articles.title: Technology startups raised nearly $300bn globally in the first half of 2021, fueling 136 new unicorns btw April and June, a quarterly record.
articles.author: Economist Staff

articles.title: The yield curve often steepens when control of the US government shifts from two parties to one, as seen in late 2016 & early 2021. Historical data shows bond yields rise with increased party confidence, as measured by the Michigan survey of consumers.

articles.title: Research by @AndrewBarr suggests the Post-9/11 GI Bill led to a 1.8% reduction in wages 7 years post-separation, indicating diminishing returns in a saturated education market.

articles.title: Firms in the top quartile of growth by sector outpace peers in intangible investments, with top growers investing 2.6 times more than low growers. @EricHazan
articles.author: Eric Hazan

articles.title: US infrastructure projects are expensive, with average costs reaching $1,601m per mile, compared to a global average of $478m. Complex designs, regulatory delays, and high labor costs contribute to these high costs. @EdwardGlaeser

articles.title: The proportion of Americans identifying as white and Christian has seen a significant decline over the past few decades, dropping from 65% in 1996 to 54% in 2006, and further to 43% by 2017.

articles.title: US electricity generation has shifted significantly since 1949, with coal dominating until 2011, natural gas surging past coal in 2016, and renewables increasing gradually.

articles.title: Increased LFP by older workers has nearly doubled, leading to a significant labor supply shock, suppressing wage growth & contributing to lower inflation, according to @BenoitMojon @Bank_for_International_Settlements.
articles.author: Benoit Mojon

articles.title: China is experiencing a significant urban contraction, with 28% of its cities showing reduced activity as indicated by dimming night lights from 2013 to 2016. @SidneyLeng reports 938 cities shrinking, the highest number globally.
articles.author: Sidney Leng

articles.title: Housing returns in the long run are comparable to equities but exhibit lower volatility and lower covariance with consumption growth, according to @MoritzSchularick @AlanMTaylor @oscarjorda @nberpubsorg.
articles.author: Òscar Jordà, Moritz Schularick, Alan Taylor

articles.title: Research by @PedroBordalo shows households moving btw US cities often pay rent closer to their city of origin due to memory-based norms influencing their choices. This affects economic behavior across different urban markets.
articles.author: Pedro Bordalo

articles.title: Only 7 Black students were admitted to Stuyvesant High School out of 895 slots, highlighting a persistent racial disparity in NYC's specialized high schools.
articles.author: Eliza Shapiro

articles.title: The US poverty rate has remained unchanged at 12-15% despite $22tn spent on anti-poverty programs since the 1960s. Economic growth has not translated into poverty reduction, with income inequality widening. @EricAHanushek
articles.author: Eric A. Hanushek

articles.title: Btw 1999-2007, every US manufacturing job lost to China created 6 manufacturing jobs in China.
articles.author: Harald Hau
articles.pub: Center for Economic and Policy Research

articles.title: @MESandbu argues that the concept of secular stagnation suggests rich economies face persistently low demand, challenging central banks & potentially justifying harmful trade policies.
articles.author: Martin Sandbu

articles.title: China's current-account surplus has vanished, now standing at just 0.4% of GDP, marking a shift towards balanced trade.
articles.author: Economist Staff

articles.title: Non-college educated white Americans born in the 1960s face significant economic challenges, with lower wages, shorter life expectancies, and higher medical costs resulting in substantial welfare costs.
articles.author: Margherita Borella

articles.title: A recent study found no significant differences in student achievement gains linked to different textbooks, challenging the Gates Foundation's curriculum-focused strategy.
articles.author: Matt Barnum

articles.title: US firms engaged in a $8tn buyback and dividend spree from 2009-2018, enough to buy all major listed firms in the UK, France, Germany, Spain, Italy, and Sweden.
articles.author: Robin Wigglesworth

articles.title: China shocks impacted US manufacturing most in late product cycle industries by 1990, with 0.25% higher unemployment rates in areas with low education & high wages.
articles.author: Katherine Eriksson

articles.title: Opioids account for 44% of the decline in male labor force participation btw 2001-2015. A 10% increase in local opioid prescription rates correlates with a 0.50 percentage point drop in employment rates for prime-age men.
articles.author: Dionissi Aliprantis, Kyle Fee, and Mark E. Schweitzer

articles.title: Black Americans have consistently been the Democrats' most loyal voting bloc, with no Democratic presidential candidate receiving less than 80% of the black vote since 1964.
articles.author: Economist Staff

articles.title: High-return firms invest significantly in growth capex and R&D, while those with lower asset returns may find returning cash to shareholders more viable. @JoannaOssinger
articles.author: Joanna Ossinger

articles.title: The US trade deficit isn't driven by domestic budget deficits but by global economic environment where foreign producers rely on US consumers to absorb excess output.
articles.author: Matthew Klein

articles.title: Less-educated men's labor force participation decline is partly driven by a new willingness to be supported by family, rather than labor demand shocks.
articles.author: Ariel Binder and John Bound

articles.title: The gap btw risky & safe interest rates has fluctuated significantly over the past decades, standing at 5.5%-points today, driven by factors such as the end of dot-com exuberance & increased demand for safe assets during the China shock.
articles.author: Brad DeLong

articles.title: @LHSummers: The natural real rate for advanced economies has declined by ~3% over the past 40yrs, now slightly above 0 in real terms. Without offsetting policies, mature economies risk secular stagnation, necessitating greater budget deficits & unconventional monetary policies.
articles.author: Larry Summers

articles.title: 1988-2017: Low-skilled workers show higher employment sensitivity to cycles. Tight labor mkts drive stronger gains for marginal workers vs overall workforce in expansions, steeper drops in downturns.
articles.author: Stephanie R. Aaronson

articles.title: The Tax Cuts and Jobs Act has significantly stimulated the U.S. economy, with CBO projecting it will cover 80% of its cost through higher-than-expected growth.

articles.title: @michaelxpettis suggests taxing imports of risk-averse capital into the U.S. rather than imposing tariffs or quotas on trade.
articles.author: Michael Pettis

articles.title: The tight labor market is proving advantageous for American workers, with real wage growth accelerating as unemployment remains low. Disadvantaged workers are benefiting from increased employment opportunities & wage gains.
articles.author: Eric Morath and Lauren Weber

articles.title: Aging workforce dampens productivity: Study projects -3-9% output decline next decade. Older worker concentration reduces individual wages, signals broader economic growth challenge.
articles.author: Adam Ozimek, Dante DeAntonio and Mark Zandi

articles.title: US healthcare spending may contribute to better cancer survival rates compared to Europe. Understanding these differences is crucial for economists and business professionals as they assess the implications of healthcare spending on national productivity and economic growth.
articles.author: Tomas Philipson, Michael Eber, Darius N. Lakdawalla, Mitra Corral, Rena Conti and Dana P. Goldman

articles.title: Manufacturing jobs have rebounded since 2010, adding nearly 1m jobs, led by transportation equipment & fabricated metals/machinery. Despite gains, base remains a fraction of 1980s level.
articles.author: Jaison Abel and Richard Deitz
articles.pub: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

articles.title: US consumers are increasingly skeptical about housing valuations, reaching levels not seen since the 2006 bubble, driven by rising mortgage rates & a recovery in house prices. @M_C_Klein

articles.title: Tax cuts implemented during the Obama-Trump era appear to have stimulated economic growth without triggering inflation, according to @AlanBlinder in the Wall Street Journal.
articles.author: Alan Blinder

articles.title: The Green New Deal is estimated to cost btw $52.6tn and $94.4tn over a decade, according to @DougHoltzEakin.
articles.author: Doug Holtz-Eakin

articles.title: @michaelxpettis US debt must rise to prevent unemployment due to its role in the global balance-of-payments system & high income inequality, as trade deficits & income inequality necessitate increased borrowing to maintain economic growth.

articles.title: NYC's $773m Renewal program, aimed at revitalizing failing schools, was terminated by Mayor de Blasio after 3 years due to insufficient progress.
articles.author: Eliza Shapiro

articles.title: Technological changes have disproportionately favored college-educated workers, exacerbating economic disparities & reducing opportunities for non-college workers in high-wage cities. @DavidAutor

articles.title: Low interest rates reduce business dynamism as market leaders gain share, while market followers cut investment to a greater degree than they do in the face of low rates.
articles.author: Ernest Liu, Atif Mian and Amir Sufi

articles.title: Germany's fiscal surplus as a share of GDP has been steadily increasing, reaching 1.7% in 2018, up from 1.2% in 2017. @Brad_Setser argues this growing surplus presents a compelling case for a significant fiscal stimulus.
articles.author: Brad Setser

articles.title: Public firms have easier access to capital during stress periods like the Great Recession, unlike private firms whose leverage declines, according to @EminDinlersoz @SebnemKalemliOzcan @HenryHyatt @VeronikaPenciakova @nberpubsorg.
articles.author: Emin Dinlersoz, Sebnem Kalemli-Ozcan, Henry Hyatt, and Veronika Penciakova

articles.title: The "genius share" of US labor income surged from 6.7% in 1984 to 31.4% in 2006, highlighting income concentration among exceptional talents. @SethGBenzell
articles.author: Seth G. Benzell

articles.title: Secrecy During WWII Reduced Commercialization/Diffusion Of Tech Change.
articles.author: Daniel Gross

articles.title: Immigrant entrepreneurs outperformed native entrepreneurs in US high-tech sectors, exhibiting higher innovation rates across various metrics, including product and process innovation, according to @JDavidBrown @nberpubs.
articles.author: J. David Brown, John Earle, Mee Jung Kim, Kyung Min Lee

articles.title: Countries with a high number of billionaires per capita often exhibit elevated levels of well-being & competitiveness, as evidenced by the 2018 Credit Suisse Global Wealth Report.
articles.author: James Pethokoukis

articles.title: According to @stlouisfed, no significant relationship exists btw mortgage borrowing growth and changes in housing stock value from 2006-18.
articles.author: William Emmons, Ana Hernández Kent and Lowell Ricketts
articles.pub: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis

articles.title: Taxing high-end consumption, not income or wealth, is a more effective approach. A 70-80% tax on luxury consumption would not deter the superrich from work or investment, @ScottSumner argues, via @TheMoneyIllusion.
articles.author: Scott Sumner

articles.title: Deficits raise 10-year Treasury yield by 18bps & ACM by 20bps, but aging demographics & high foreign demand for US Treasuries have offset this effect since 2001.
articles.author: Ernie Tedeschi

articles.title: 2008-12 recession drove +1.4m SSDI claims (+16.5%), 400k awards. Total cost: $58.7bn ($2.96bn admin + $55.73bn benefits) + Medicare brings to $97.4bn. 53% approved on appeal, 8.9% of new beneficiaries recession-induced
articles.author: Nicole Maestas, Kathleen J. Mullen and Alexander Strand

articles.title: The exploration of the amplituhedron by Arkani-Hamed & Trnka shifts understanding of physics, focusing on discovering the mathematical question from which all answers flow @NatalieWolchover The.
articles.author: Natalie Wolchover

articles.title: Negative-yielding government bonds have surged 21% since October to $13tn, reflecting waning economic confidence in Europe and Japan. @TheRealDFK
articles.author: Daniel Kruger

articles.title: Debt can't go to infinity - it can't exceed total wealth.
articles.author: Paul Krugman

articles.title: CB Insights says 50 start-ups are on path to $1bn valuation, driven by financial health & market size. 315 unicorns now, up from 131 in 2015.

articles.title: Restricting buybacks could lead to increased reinvestment, but often in projects that fail to meet hurdle rates, as companies initially opted for buybacks due to a lack of lucrative opportunities.
articles.author: Aswath Damodaran

articles.pub: New York State Division of the Budget

articles.title: Foreign ownership of US debt has declined from 58% at 2008 peak to < 40%.
articles.author: Daniel Kruger

articles.author: Michael Durkheimer

articles.title: @Brad_Setser The global distribution of corporations' offshore profits remains largely unchanged, with over half still booked in low-tax nations. The TCJA has not effectively altered the incentives for booking profits offshore.

articles.title: The debt held by the public as a percentage of GDP has surpassed historical averages, driven by increased government spending and slower economic growth, according to @CBOStaff.
articles.author: CBO Staff

articles.title: Wealth taxes have fallen out of favor globally. By 2017, only 4 high-income countries retained wealth taxes, down from 12 in 1990. @TimothyDTaylor
articles.author: Timothy Taylor

articles.title: A progressive wealth tax could target the top 0.1% of wealth holders, who own 20% of US wealth. A 2% tax on wealth btw $50m & $1bn, and 3% on wealth above $1bn, could generate $2.75tn over a decade.

articles.title: NYC ride-share drivers can earn $72,000 annually from rides, with additional income from services like Cargo and Play Octopus, totaling around $75,000 before taxes and expenses.

articles.title: Support for Medicare for All drops 19% when Americans learn it could eliminate private insurance or increase taxes.
articles.author: Jonathan Martin

articles.title: Design in nature evolves toward facilitating the movement of whatever flows through it.
articles.author: J. Peder Zane

articles.title: @FareedZakaria, Washington Post: Despite widespread pessimism, global poverty has seen a remarkable decline, supporting the current world order. Since 1990, 1bn people have moved out of extreme poverty, reducing the global share from 36% to 10%.

articles.title: Income inequality significantly impacts health expenditures, with disparities in mean household disposable income by decile evident btw the US and Northern Europe. @R.C.A.
articles.author: R.C.A.

articles.title: Analysis of 1.2m U.S. inventors reveals that the top 1% earn over 22% of total inventors' income, with high correlation btw income and social impact. Reducing top income tax rates has limited potential to boost aggregate innovation, as it primarily influences those already exposed to innovation.

articles.title: Austin's wage growth outpaced the national average from 2001-2017, reflecting its robust economic expansion and attractiveness to tech & business sectors.

articles.author: Mathur

articles.title: Data from @StatisticsCanada reveals the top 25 countries ranked by Actual Individual Consumption (AIC) per capita, highlighting significant disparities in living standards and economic well-being.

articles.title: Data shows countries with higher real disposable income per capita spend more on healthcare, yet this doesn't always correlate with better health outcomes.
articles.author: Random Critical Analysis

articles.title: Data shows countries with similar GDP per capita can have vastly different health spending levels. Real AIC per capita is a more accurate predictor of health expenditure than Real GDP per capita.

articles.title: Critics of Piketty & Saez highlight errors in their methodology, such as excluding gov't transfer payments from national income calculations. Adjusted figures reveal a much flatter curve in income distribution.

articles.title: RAND Education Chief Darleen Opfer Gate's Education Initiative "Didn't Make An Impact".
articles.author: Frederick Hess

articles.title: The US leads global unicorns with 151 valued at $556bn, 51% of $1.087tn total. Europe has 33 unicorns valued at $72.97bn (13% of US total). China has 86 unicorns valued at $278bn (50% of US total). @CBInsights Insights Staff.
articles.author: Insights Staff

articles.title: Contrary to Piketty and Saez’s recent claim, new @nberpub research by Matt Smith @dannyyagan @omzidar and Eric Zwick provides persuasive evidence that a majority of top 1% and 0.1% income is labor income—chiefly business owners.
articles.author: Matthew Smith, Danny Yagan, Owen M. Zidar, Eric Zwick

articles.author: Gordon Green

articles.title: A top marginal tax rate above 30% may suboptimize social welfare by stifling idea creation, reducing GDP & overall income. A utilitarian welfare function suggests a top tax rate of just 29% maximizes social welfare. @CharlesJones
articles.author: Charles Jones

articles.title: Raising top marginal tax rate from 50% to 75% in the US could generate 2.5% of GDP in revenue but reduce innovation & lower GDP per person by 6% in the long run.
articles.author: Charles Jones

articles.title: According to @GeorgeBorjas @nberpubs, the influx of industrial robots has been modest but is poised to surpass the impact of additional immigration on labor markets.

articles.author: Daniel J. Wilson
articles.pub: Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco

articles.title: Inventors are significantly less likely to locate in states with higher taxes, with an elasticity to the net-of-tax rate of 0.11 for local inventors and 1.23 for non-local inventors.
articles.author: Stefanie Stantcheva

articles.title: NY State cutting personal income tax rate on top 1% of earners in 2006 increased net inflow of star scientists to NYS by +3% a year.

articles.title: The share of working-age Americans with a college degree is now 20% higher in urban areas than rural ones, a significant increase from a 5% gap in 1970.

articles.title: Btw 1991 and 2007, US exports generated significant job opportunities but were slightly insufficient to offset job losses from Chinese imports, netting a 0.2-0.3m job loss.

articles.title: Feenstra finds a fall in labor demand due to trade but a rise in labor demand due to service exports overall positive for job growth.
articles.author: Robert C. Feenstra and Akira Sasahara

articles.title: Trade's impact on domestic employment is minimal, as evidenced by research showing a net loss of only 200,000 jobs from 1991 to 2007 due to Chinese imports, offset by 3.3m export-driven job gains. @MichaelRStrain
articles.author: Michael R. Strain

articles.title: David Autor's analysis highlights the decline of middle-skilled jobs that offered higher wages & benefits, but low-skilled service & retail jobs lack these advantages. Housing expenses surge, low-skilled workers face low incomes & high living costs.
articles.author: Emily Badger and Quoctrung Bui

articles.title: Sweden's market reforms: Govt spend cut 70% to <50% GDP, debt 80% to 41%, corp tax -6pts to 22%. Results: Growth +1pt vs EU since 1995, GDP/capita now at 85% of US. Ranks 15th in economic freedom.
articles.author: Jesús Fernández-Villaverde

articles.title: US market capitalization of listed domestic firms rose from 90.709% of GDP in 1995 to 165.651% by 2017, according to @WorldBank. France & Germany saw similar growth, underscoring the growing importance of stock markets in national economies.
articles.author: World Bank

articles.title: Highly educated women are increasingly balancing career and family life, with childlessness rates declining and family sizes growing. By 2014, 22% of women aged 40-44 with a master's degree or higher were childless, down from 30% in 1994.
articles.author: Gretchen Livingston

articles.title: US women are more likely to have children than a decade ago, despite delaying childbirth. Women with higher education are waiting longer but ultimately achieving higher fertility rates.
articles.author: Gretchen Livingston

articles.title: Automation & globalization reshape job landscapes. Middle-skill jobs fell from 60% to 45% of total employment, while high-skill jobs rose from 25% to 35% from 1980 to 2015 @DavidAutor
articles.author: David Autor

articles.title: U.S. exports of manufactured goods to China have declined as a share of both Chinese and U.S. GDP over the past five years, indicating a shift in trade dynamics. @Brad_Setser
articles.author: Brad Setser

articles.title: Taxes on the rich were not much higher in the 1950s. Despite top tax rates ranging from 91% to 70%, the superrich paid an average federal tax rate of less than 30%. @JamesPethokoukis
articles.author: James Pethokoukis

articles.title: The unemployment rate fell significantly from 2014-2018, leading to a tighter labor market & increased Average Hourly Earnings. Employers absorbed higher labor costs, potentially reducing profit margins, & sought efficiency gains to offset rising costs.

articles.title: Taxpayers were billed $27.2tn for federal services that actually cost $35.6tn, a 26% undercharge adding over $9tn to the national debt.

articles.title: Data on total hours worked in OECD countries reveals that employment is a poor proxy for cyclical labor input, with 50% of labor adjustment occurring along the intensive margin. @LeeEOhanian, @nberpub.

articles.title: The gig economy's rise was overstated, with nontraditional jobs increasing by only 1-2% points over the last decade, not 5% as previously estimated, according to @joshzumbrun @WSJ.

articles.title: Marriott's Return on Assets (ROA) from 2006 to 2020 reflects significant fluctuations, driven by global travel demand and internal strategic shifts. ROA peaked at 8.5% in 2007, then declined to 3.2% in 2009, before recovering to 7% from 2016 to 2019.

articles.title: The Trump administration's rollback of disparate impact principle could alter civil rights enforcement, impacting voting rights, employment statutes & Fair Housing Act. Disparate impact allows discrimination to be identified based on outcomes rather than intent.

articles.title: Hispanic workers are disproportionately assigned to lower-skill occupations due to lower educational attainment compared to non-Hispanic workers. Controlling for education, age, and gender largely eliminates the Hispanic/non-Hispanic labor market gaps.

articles.author: Mary Amiti
articles.pub: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

articles.title: High-skilled workers started working longer hours in the mid-1980s, increasing the value of their time & raising commuting costs, driving a demand for central city locations & initiating urban gentrification.

articles.title: The Equity Risk Premium (ERP) in January 2019 was 5.96%, highlighting the inherent risk associated with equities.
articles.author: Aswath Damodaran

articles.title: The Supreme Court has the potential to restrain government overreach by adhering to originalism, reviving the nondelegation doctrine, ending judicial deference to agency interpretations, and ensuring executive power remains vested in the president.

articles.title: The "China Shock" hardened existing racial and religious views among whites, rather than altering attitudes broadly. @MichaelStrain, American Enterprise Institute.

articles.title: US energy production may be lower than forecasted due to unsustainable economics in fracking. Analysis of 16,000 wells shows 2/3 of 2014-2017 projections were overly optimistic.

articles.title: Corporate R&D investment increases entrepreneurial spawning by 18.7% with a one standard deviation rise in R&D, according to @SabrinaTHowell. However, these spillovers do not harm the parent company.
articles.author: Sabrina T. Howell

articles.title: The Tax Cuts and Jobs Act has been criticized for its economic implications, particularly by economist @PaulKrugman. A third of US corporate profits are owned by foreign nationals, making the US poorer overall. @PaulKrugman -.

articles.title: James Watson's remarks on race have sparked debate, impacting scientific & economic communities. Diversity in STEM fields is crucial for innovation & growth, with diverse teams increasing productivity by up to 35%.

articles.author: Elizabeth Currid-Halkett

articles.title: The Obama administration's guidance letters led schools to prioritize racial parity over safety, resulting in increased school violence & disorder, particularly in cities like Los Angeles & Chicago.

articles.title: A Kansas City Fed study highlights that initial population size has become a crucial factor in determining employment and population growth since 2000.

articles.author: Matthew Klein

articles.title: US capex growth is driven by 20% yoy increase in energy capex, which makes up only 8% of total S&E capex. Excluding energy, tangible capex growth is under 5%. @FrancisRybinski @HarrisKere, Aegon Assert Management.

articles.title: Capex among S&P 500 firms surged in Q1, driven by tech sector growth. Alphabet's capex nearly tripled to $7.3bn, but shares fell 4.8% post-earnings as investors questioned spending sustainability.

articles.title: The labor share of GDP has significantly declined in 42 of 59 countries studied, including China, India, and Mexico, as documented by economists @LoukasKarabarbounis and @BrentNeiman.
articles.author: Loukas Karabarbounis and Brent Neiman

articles.title: Digital technologies have driven a decline in labor's share of GDP in 42 of 59 countries studied, as firms shift from labor to capital due to cheaper investment goods. @MichaelSpence
articles.author: Michael Spence

articles.title: The financial crisis has left a lasting impact on the U.S. economy, with a 13.3% GDP output gap primarily driven by structural issues.
articles.author: Bob Hall

articles.title: Wealth inequality drivers quantified: Estate tax elimination raises output +0.35%, capital +0.87%, Gini index 0.79→0.80. Model shows circumstances, not inheritance, drive US earnings/wealth gaps
articles.author: Castaneda, Ana and Javier Diaz-Gimenez and Jose-Victor Rios-Rull

articles.title: Income concentration in the U.S. has shown significant fluctuations over the decades, with the top 1% seeing their income share rise from 10% in 1979 to 24% in 2007, before dropping sharply during the 2007-2009 financial crisis and nearing the 2007 peak by 2012.
articles.author: Scott Winship

articles.title: Income inequality rose markedly btw 1980-2010, but consumption inequality remained stable. Leisure inequality also widened, with low-educated men gaining 2.5 hours of leisure per week by 2007, while high-educated men saw a 1.2-hour decline.
articles.author: Erik Hurst

articles.title: The social safety net saw increased transfers across income groups during the Great Recession, but the increase was not strongly progressive within low-income populations.
articles.author: Robert Moffitt

articles.title: Uncertainty Traps by @FajgelbaumPablo @EdouardSchael @MathieuTD, @nberpub: Feedback loops in economic systems can amplify uncertainty, leading to prolonged recessions even when fundamentals are strong.
articles.author: Fajgelbaum, Pablo, Edouard Schael and Mathieu Taschereau-Dumouchel

articles.title: Performance pay has significantly contributed to wage inequality, explaining 24% of male wage variance growth from the late 1970s to early 1990s. @LemieuxThomas @MacLeodW @DanielParent
articles.author: Lemieux, Thomas, MacLeod, W Bentley, and Daniel Parent

articles.title: Intergenerational earnings mobility varies significantly across countries, with the US exhibiting lower mobility compared to Nordic countries and the UK. In the US, 50% of parental income advantages are passed to children, compared to 20-30% in Nordic countries.
articles.author: Robin Naylor, Eva Osterbacka, Andres Bjorklund, Tor Eriksson

articles.title: The foreign-born population in the U.S. is approaching 37m, representing 12% of the total population, according to recent @USCensusBureau data. This demographic shift highlights the role of immigration in shaping the nation's economic landscape.
articles.author: U.S. Census Bureau

articles.title: Greg Mankiw's Blog @GregMankiw: From 1979 to 2007, median real income for tax units increased by only 3.2%, but 36.7% when adjusting for household size & employer-provided health insurance.
articles.author: Greg Mankiw

articles.title: Demographic shifts are poised to significantly impact median income levels in the U.S., with projections indicating a decline in the average American's income position. @BillSteele
articles.author: Bill Steele

articles.title: Disabled workers have risen from 1 in 35 in 1992 to 1 in 16 today, totaling 8.7m workers. @EnjoyedlunchTwo @EnjoyedlunchTwo attachments on disability.
articles.author: SSA Staff

articles.title: Cosmic rays at highest energies might be created by unknown particles, possibly under the influence of novel particle physics.
articles.author: David Jones

articles.title: A minivan size asteroid explodes over California, triggering a modern-day 'gold rush' for meteorite fragments, potentially boosting local economies through tourism & scientific investment.
articles.author: Discovery News Staff
